title: Adding a second relay breaks _handleGiftWrap
description: if I set up subscriptions on two relays in ndk_service then `_handleGiftWrap` is never called

title: Set up production horcrux relay
description: Set up a relay at relay.horcruxbackup.com that will be the default relay on all horcrux vaults.

title: Ship public beta
description: Once I have completed all other beads tagged version:0.9 I will need to make a build available to anyone who wants to beta test the app. Subtasks will include uploading to the various app stores, writing descriptions, setting up support channels, etc.

title: Export Vault Contents after Recovery
description: We need to give the user the vault contents as a file and allow them to share it to another app using the system share sheet after tappign the "Open Vault" button at the end of a successful recovery.

title: Keyboard avoidance for steward name
description: When adding a steward on Android, when the name field is focused, the keyboard comes up and covers it.

title: RowButton overlaps app switcher bar on Android
description: On most screens the RowButton overlaps the horizontal bar shown at the bottom of the Android app that is used for switching apps. We need to add some margin like we do for the same bar on iPhone.

title: Refactor Nostr event formats
description: Our format for custom nostr events has grown as-needed but we need to take a step back and evaulate it. Make sure we are using consistent naming and keeping things as simple as possible with room to improve in the future.

title: Refactor Data Layer
description: Our storage layer writes json objects to SharedPreferences. This was fine to start but I am worried about concurrency and integrity issues. Perhaps it's time for a real database. Also Probably should factor out BackupConfig, maybe add StewardedVault or something for the version stewards get. Rename Shard to Share.

comments.author: mplorentz
comments.text: Make sure we end up with a single source of truth for stewards and we don't have to check in the shards as well as the backup config.

title: Manage recovery button does not show
description: steps to reproduce: delete vault contents, start recovery, then set new vault contents -> manage recovery button does not show up.

title: Clean up Relay Management screen
description: This has weird buttons that dont' really match behavior. Really we should be able to see what relays are connected and manually add/remove some to scan.

title: Test vault deletion
description: We need to test behavior around vault deletion on the owner and the steward size and create beads for any follow up work to smooth out the experience

title: Populate Learn More link on vault creation screen
description: Currently this link goes to nothing.

title: Cleanup Invitation Screen UX
description: Fix "Invitation" and "You've been invited" right next to each other on the invitation screen. Probably should inline the names on this screen too.

title: Fix App Title Bar Overflow
description: We use large-font AppBar instances on almost every screen. Unfortunately on phone screens the text is often cut off. We need to fix this so that the entire title is readable even on small phones. This is a major design decision. We could try to make the text wrap or we could lower the font size. Brainstorm other solutions and maybe mock them all up.

title: Use bead ID in worktree
description: Cursor and claude create worktrees at their own paths. Cursor seems to use a random ID and claude uses the branch name. Let's standardize on the bead ID. Add instructions where appropriate to tell them to do this.

title: mol-do-work
description:
Simple work formula — read the bead, do what it says, close it.

This is the minimal work lifecycle for coding agents. No git branching,
no worktree isolation, no refinery handoff. The agent reads the bead's
description, implements the solution in the current working directory,
and closes the bead when done.

Use this for demos and simple single-agent workflows. For production
multi-agent setups with branch isolation and merge review, use
mol-polecat-work instead.

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | caller | The work bead ID assigned to this agent |

title: Read assignment, implement, and close
description:
You have been assigned a work bead. Read it, do the work, and close it.

**1. Read your assignment:**
```bash
bd show horcrux_app-mkf
```

Read the bead's title and description carefully. This is your task.

**2. Implement the solution:**

Do exactly what the bead describes. Follow existing codebase conventions.
Make atomic, focused commits as you work:
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description>"
```

**3. Close the bead when done:**
```bash
bd update horcrux_app-mkf --status=closed --notes "Done: <brief summary of what you did>"
```

If you get stuck or need clarification, check your inbox:
```bash
gc mail inbox
```

**Exit criteria:** The work described in the bead is complete and the bead is closed.

title: Signal completion
description:
Work is done. Signal the controller to reclaim this session:

```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
```

Run this command and nothing else.

description: create a vault with empty contents. Then after adding stewards you get a bad error message about failing to create shamir's shares

title: Multi-relay vaults

description: make recovery status screen say Pending Response instead of just Pending

description: The app toast messages often cover RowButtons, and the default color for them blends in too much with the background.

title: Fix switch color contrast
description: the contrast between a switch that is turned on vs turned off is too low (i.e. on recovery plan screen.)

title: Login does not download vaults
description: Currently if you login with a key during onboarding we don't scan any of your relays for existing vaults. We should probably scan the horcrux one and prompt users to enter any others they know they use, mabye showing a versino of teh Relay Management screen during onboarding.

title: Rethink "Delete local copy" button text
description: Come up with another idea for this button. it's not clear what it will do with the current title.

title: Make a Cursor Skill/Command for working on beads
description: Cursor seems to be getting tripped up regularly and not fully updating statuses or fully cleaning up and closing beads when completed. I want to be able to type /bead <bead-id> in chat and have the agent read the bead and take the appropriate action to move it to the next human-review status, whatever that might be. If we're just starting then we need a worktree, branch, plan, etc. If CI has failed then we need to fix it. If the PR has been approved we need to merge it. If the pr has been merged we need to clean up, etc. It would be great if it worked with claude as well.

title: Update agent rules to require bead ID in PR descriptions
description: Amend AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, CONTRIBUTING.md, and any PR template so contributors and AI agents always include the relevant bd issue ID in every PR description when work is tracked in bd.
acceptance_criteria: Documentation clearly states PR descriptions must include the bead ID; agent-facing instructions agree across files.
notes:

Also updated .cursor/rules/beads.mdc (alwaysApply). Commit dad674e on doc/bead-id-in-pr-description in worktree.
Opened PR https://github.com/mplorentz/horcrux/pull/124 (stage pr-open).
PR #124 merged to main. Closing bead.
close_reason: Merged via https://github.com/mplorentz/horcrux/pull/124 — documentation and templates now require bd issue ID in PR descriptions when work is bead-tracked.

title: Block duplicate recovery response when opening answered push notification
description:
Opening a recovery-request push notification from the OS can navigate to a recovery request the user already responded to. The screen still allows submitting a response again.

Add handling similar to the other push/deep-link guard: detect already-responded state (or stale notification) and show an informative dialog instead of the full respond flow, so stewards cannot accidentally double-submit.
acceptance_criteria: Tapping an OS notification for a recovery request the user already responded to shows a clear dialog (no second submit path). Fresh unanswered requests behave unchanged.

title: Merge PR via gh api when gh pr merge fails (multi-worktree main)
description:
When another git worktree already has main checked out, `gh pr merge` can fail with: fatal: 'main' is already used by worktree at ... Merge on GitHub without touching local refs:

```bash
gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<number>/merge -X PUT \\
  -f merge_method=squash \\
  -f commit_title='Short title (#123)'
```

Use merge_method merge or rebase if the repo prefers. Optionally delete the remote branch afterward via the GitHub UI or refs API.
notes: Observed 2026-05-01 merging mplorentz/horcrux PR121 from horcrux_wt_pr121 while main lived in horcrux_app worktree.

title: Agent playbook: clean bead labels/metadata when closing PR-linked work
description:
Use this checklist whenever you close a bead that tracked GitHub work (branch / worktree / PR). Goal: no stale workflow labels or obsolete paths on closed issues.

## When this applies
- Bead has (or had) metadata like `branch`, `worktree`, or maps to a PR.
- You are closing after merge, or abandoning with a documented reason.

## Close the bead
- `bd close <id> -r "..."` with a concrete reason (PR URL or plain-language outcome).

## Right after close — Beads hygiene (required)
See also `.beads/STAGES.md` (remove old `stage:*` / `blocked-on:*` when workflow moves).

1. **Strip stale workflow labels** on the closed issue so it does not look "in progress":
   - Remove `blocked-on:agent`, `blocked-on:ci`, `blocked-on:human` if no longer true.
   - Remove active `stage:*` labels (`stage:agent-fixing`, `stage:pr-open`, `stage:implementing`, etc.). Closed beads should not carry labels that imply ongoing work.

2. **Post-merge cleanup label**: If you temporarily use `stage:cleanup-needed` for worktree/branch deletion, **remove it once cleanup is done** — do not leave it on a closed bead.

3. **Metadata** — keep what is still true, clear what is not:
   - Set `pr_url` to the full PR URL when known: `bd update <id> --set-metadata pr_url=https://github.com/.../pull/N`
   - After you delete the local branch and remove the git worktree, clear branch/worktree metadata: `bd update <id> --set-metadata branch= --set-metadata worktree=`

4. **Notes** — append one line of factual cleanup for future you: `bd update <id> --append-notes "..."` (e.g. worktree path removed, local branch deleted).

5. **Optional comment** — if verification matters, `bd comment <id> "..."` with CI commands or evidence.

## Same session — Git hygiene (often paired)
- Remove dedicated worktrees when the branch is merged or abandoned: `git worktree remove <path>` then `git worktree prune`.
- Delete merged local branches: `git branch -d <branch>`.
- In app repos, `flutter clean` in that worktree if you are retiring it.

Agents should treat steps under "Right after close" as part of closing the loop, not optional polish.
notes: Closed wontfix; see horcrux_app-v6e for automated bead workflow and .beads/STAGES.md for stages.
close_reason: Wontfix: bead close hygiene is covered by horcrux_app-v6e (/bead workflow skill) and existing STAGES.md / agent rules.

title: Remove archon
description: Remove all references to the archon tool from the repo. I am no longer using it.

value: Always use --json with bd commands when invoking them programmatically (list, show, search, ready, etc). Reason: structured output is more reliable to parse than human-formatted output and avoids losing fields. How to apply: append --json to any bd read command unless the user is asking for human-readable output for themselves.
